subject: Sociology
title: Sociology vs Common Sense
description: A quick win: understand Sociology vs Common Sense.

hook.text: Everyone knows that opposites attract, right? Sociology tested it. Turns out, similarity predicts relationships better.

buildup.text: Common sense often feels obvious after the fact. But many folk beliefs crumble under systematic investigation.

discovery.text: Sociology uses evidence to challenge assumptions. What feels true and what is true often diverge sharply.

twist.text: People also believe poverty comes from laziness. Data shows structural barriers explain far more than effort alone.

climax.text: Sociology's power is replacing comfortable assumptions with uncomfortable evidence about how society works.

punchline.text: Common sense explains everything — and gets half of it wrong.

quiz.question: Why does sociology challenge common sense?
